description: To gather crops or other cultivated growth when it has reached maturity. 'Muraki' is the culmination of the agricultural cycle—the reward for patient tending, the moment when what was sown is finally reaped. It carries both labor and celebration.
sound_symbolism: The word moves through the harvest arc. 'Mu' begins with nasal inwardness, assessing readiness. Then 'ra' reaches with flowing motion, arms gathering. Finally 'ki' cuts with velar precision, the ripe separated from the growing.
grammatical_notes: 'mia lureko muraki.' (I harvest fruit.) The noun sense, a harvest, is the event noun the rule provides: 'welao muraki' (a good harvest). Completes the cycle begun by 'thophe' (sow); the growing crop is kept with 'temoe' (tend).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: We harvest what we've sown—karma ripening into present experience. The reaping makes its causes visible: the sowing, the tending, the seasons between.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Harvest time structures traditional calendars—communities gathered, labor shared, abundance celebrated. Harvest knowledge determines survival.
